title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack with beers inspired by Boston athletes Derrick White and Garrett Crochet.
The pack also features beers inspired by Julian Edelman and Zdeno Chara, offering a lager and a pilsner respectively.
A public launch event with the athletes is scheduled for April 4 at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray will run his 54th Boston Marathon with his children Max, Luke, Elle, and astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray will still fulfill his duties as race director, coordinating the start before joining the run with his family.
Max and Elle McGillivray are raising funds for charity, with astronaut Sunita Williams running on the Finish Strong Foundation’s team.

title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the first half of the Celtics' loss to the San Antonio Spurs on Tuesday.
Jayson Tatum criticized referees, questioning the quick ejection in a national prime time game with top NBA teams playing.
Derrick White and coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown's frustration, labeling the second technical foul as unwarranted.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, from Encinitas, Calif., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his mother, Janice.
Hebert will wear a shirt resembling lilac in honor of his late mother's adventurous spirit and love for lilacs.
Janice, a transplanted New Englander, enjoyed adventures such as hang-gliding, skydiving, and ziplining throughout her life.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Authorities investigate potential vandalism of geotubes on Nantucket's Sconset Bluff, believed to be intentionally cut.
The Sconset Beach Preservation Fund reported the incident and posted a $10,000 reward for information leading to an arrest.
Strong winter storms further damaged geotubes, originally installed in 2014, putting them at risk of complete failure.

title: ICE detains Marlborough 14-year-old, apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rep. Lori Trahan claims ICE detained a 14-year-old girl from Marlborough to coerce her father to surrender for deportation.
The girl, with no criminal history and not enrolled in local schools, was allegedly used as bait during an overnight attempt.
Trahan demands the girl's immediate release, drawing parallels to a similar incident involving a child in Minnesota.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Easthampton resident Natalie Graves, 34, and her aunt, Linda Dewey, 65, were killed in a triple homicide in Utah.
Suspect arrested for killing three women, including Graves, after stealing a vehicle and fleeing to a trailhead.
A GoFundMe for Graves' husband has raised over $38,000 to help with expenses following her death.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Procter & Gamble will invest $1 billion to build a new Gillette headquarters in South Boston, replacing the current location.
The new headquarters on 232 A St. will include a Grooming Headquarters and Technical Innovation Center in a 324,315-square-foot space.
Gillette's manufacturing operations will move to a 200,000-square-foot facility in Andover, initiating a transition for hundreds of employees.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed Dre'Mont Jones to bolster their edge rush, releasing K'Lavon Chaisson.
Jones, formerly with Baltimore, had seven sacks and 24 QB hits in a career-best 2025.
He brings versatility with size at 6'3, 281 lbs, and has played multiple NFL positions.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston is closing on March 29, citing economic challenges.
The closure affects 56 workers who unionized less than a month ago with UNITE HERE Local 26.
The union claims the closure is retaliatory, following a recent successful vote to unionize workers at Seamark.
